observations.recommendation: 1. With the foregoing observations in the planning and budgeting of the BGCM Fund, we recommended and Management agreed for the Local Development Council (LDC), together with the Local Finance Council (LFC), to ensure that all appropriations are properly supported with plans. observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) implement strictly the one-time
cleansing of PPE account balances and adhere to requirements as provided in the COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated 31 January 2020 e.g preparation of necessary accounting
entries and disclosures, (2) require Inventory Committee to regularly conduct physical
count or inventory taking of LGU’s PPEs under procedural guidelines enunciated in the
same COA Circular No. 2020-006, and (3) require Accounting and Property Units to
reconcile their records based on the RPCPPE accomplished by the Inventory
Committee.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the General Service Office,
Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Assessor’s Office, to exhaust all possible
means to document the legality of ownership of the Land through a Certificate of Title
in the name of the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) coordinate with the Municipal
Engineering Office and obtain necessary details related to Road Networks; and (2)
adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 particularly in the accounting and
reporting guidelines of the Road Network account.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. require the Municipal Engineering Office to religiously prepare and submit
monitoring report as to the status of the implementation of their various PPAs,
particularly those funded by the National Government Agencies;
2. require the Municipal Accounting Office to (i) conduct regular monitoring and
analysis of fund transfers to ensure that these are liquidated within the
prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose, and prepare the
schedule of fund transfers every quarter; (ii) reconcile the unliquidated fund
transfers between the source and implementing government entities, prepare the
adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of the
balances; and (iii) return to the SA any unused balance and refund of
disallowance, if any, upon completion of the project;
3. take necessary and appropriate actions and settle the impediments on the
ongoing and suspended projects to expedite the completion of the same.
4. establish and implement an effective and efficient project monitoring mechanism
through a policy or memorandum, or any issuance that would apply, to avoid the
recurrence of the same observation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. provide the additional supplemental budget to cover the lacking appropriation
of 20% LDF.
2. ensure that mandatory allocations of 20% LDF are properly observed during
budget preparations.
3. ensure that mandatory allocations of budgets are properly observed and
reviewed before endorsement/approval of any appropriation ordinance.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management agreed
the following:
1. The Municipal Development Council as headed by the Local Chief Executive
and assisted by the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
intensify monitoring and enforcing of implementation of eligible LDF PPAs in
order to ensure that the LDF is optimally utilized.
2. The management to communicate to BLGU as to necessity and utilization of
“Aid to Barangay” projects. Fast track submission and implementation of
developmental projects proposals and POW.
3. The management to communicate to PLGU as to the continuance of the
counterpart projects and status of the expected subsidy, otherwise
appropriate the funds to other relevant projects, as necessary.
4. Henceforth, the Local Finance Committee to have a periodic assessment of
completed projects for timely reversion and appropriation of any excess funds
thereon.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management agreed
on the following:
5. For the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC)
ensure that the amount included in its Annual Plan and Budget conforms with
the mandatory allocation of 30% Quick Response Fund and 70% Prevention
and Mitigation Fund; and
6. For the Budget Officer to conduct a thorough review of the budget proposals
by various Departments/Offices to ensure its compliance with budgetary
requirements

We recommended and Management agreed on the following courses of action:
1) For the local sanggunian to observe the proper authorization of any
changes in the Annual Budget in accordance with the provisions under the
Local Government Code and its implementing rules and regulations
particularly on the enactment of Appropriation Ordinance for
circumstances that warrants a Supplemental Budget; and
2) For the Municipal Budget Officer, as the one who is certifying on the
existence of available appropriation, to ensure that the expenditures are
properly covered by an approved appropriation and authority to disburse
for its particular purpose.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit all of the lacking or deficient
documentary requirements as noted in Working Paper 1 (Annex C) and necessary
justifications if warranted. Henceforth, ensure that the guidelines set forth in the
procurement under the emergency cases are followed thoroughly while the State of
Calamity is not lifted yet and financial transactions are supported with complete and
proper documentary requirements prior to payment.

1. Create an Inventory Committee to implement the proper conduct of inventory taking of all LGU owned Property, Plant, and Equipment and preparation of Physical Inventory Plan guided by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to ascertain the existence, completeness, and condition of the PPE accounts.

2. Secure the supporting documents and draw the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to record the equivalent depreciation expenses and adjustments to all the affected accounts. Henceforth, properly recognize annual depreciation of Property, Plant, and Equipment based on the guidelines set under

1. 1. For the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to formulate a comprehensive multi-sectoral development plan that directly supports the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program. Henceforth, during planning phase, the LDC should ensure that all charges against 20% Development Plan are properly supported with specific, measurable, attainable, realistic and time-bounded programs, projects and activities.

2. For the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and Local Finance Committee (LFC) to assess all savings from completed projects and evaluate the implementability of projects that remained unimplemented for several years and consider its reversion and re-appropriation to other priority development projects in compliance with the guidelines on the utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation: 4. Local budget, plans and goals shall, as far as practicable, be harmonized with national development plans, goals and strategies in order to optimize the utilization of resources and to avoid duplication in the use of fiscal and physical resources.
1. Henceforth, enhance the internal control and employ additional mechanisms in the distribution of financial aids to avoid the incurrence of delays in the future releases and payouts.
2. To ensure that the inter-agency funds' utilization and corresponding liquidation adhere to the prescribed guidelines.

1. For the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee to ensure that all programs/projects/activities (PPAs) and expenses funded by the Municipality are incorporated in the Annual Investment Program or Supplemental Investment Program and duly approved by the local sanggunian.

2. For the Municipal Budget Office to submit justification on why a duly approved and reviewed Supplemental Budget was not prepared and submitted for the re-programmed LDRRM PPAs. Henceforth, ensure that all changes in the Annual Budget of the LGU are covered by either Budget Augmentation or Supplemental Budget, as circumstances warrants, through an approved Appropriation Ordinance.

accounts.

under IPSAS17andother relevant COAissuances.
3. Obtain necessary details for the recorded Local Road Network from Municipal

008.

For theGeneral Service Office,andMunicipalAccountingOffice:

Accordingly, for the entry made in the General Fund, the Due to Other Funds account
should have been recorded to recognize advances to Trust Fund for LGU Counterpart and
recognize the Transfer for Project Equity Share/ LGU Counterpart upon fulfillment of the
conditionof thetransfer.

In its official reply letter, the Management recognized the variances noted and further
detailed the reasons for such discrepancies, among which includes, the erroneous entries and
improper account classification. As a corrective measure, the Municipal Accountant made
the adjusting entries on the reciprocal accounts, Due to and Due from Other Funds. Thus, the
corrected balance oftheaccounts as ofJune30,2021,amounted to₱4,598,047.52.

In effect, the Due from Other Funds account is understated thereby affecting the
reliability and correctness of the reported balances in the financial statements.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed
that henceforth, conduct a regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and
ensure that adjusting entries are recognized on a timely basis. Upon transfer of fund
from General Fund to Trust Fund, for LGU counterpart of projects, recognize the
proper reciprocal accounts (Due to and Due from Other Funds). Accordingly, upon
completion of the purpose or fulfillment of the condition of the transfer, recognize the
Transfers for Project Equity Share /LGU Counterpart and Transfers from General
Fund of LGU Counterpart/Equity Share accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
execute the following:
a. Strictly direct all the Head of Offices and Accountable Officers to stop
requesting a cash advance in a form that will accommodate the operation and
activities of other Offices;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to review and examine all unliquidated cash
advances and issue a demand letter to the Accountable Officers with due and
demandable cash advances for the immediate liquidation thereof; and
c. Direct all the Accountable Officers (employees and officials) who were granted
with cash advances to strictly observe the deadline set to submit the required
liquidation documents to the Municipal Accounting Office within the prescribed
period.

The Audit Team considered Management’s compliance and commitments and
thus recommended the following to which Management consequently agreed:
a.) For the Municipal Accountant to submit the adjusting entries made on the
adjustments to Accounts Payable and financial statements reflecting its
corrected amount;
b.) Henceforth, regularly monitor the outstanding balances in the Accounts
Payable account to include only the bonafide obligations with complete
supporting documents. Ensure that erroneous entries and excess of actual
claims are adjusted in the books upon discovery to reliably present the
liability account of the Municipality.

We acknowledged Management’s partial compliance with the observations.
Thus, to further correct and improve the noted deficiencies, we recommended and
Management agreed on the following:
a. For the Municipal Accountant to continue the conduct of reconciliation and
analysis of all outstanding withheld taxes to BIR to ascertain its existence and
validity. Prepare adjusting/reconciliation entries, if it warrants; and
b. For the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare a
remittance list and remit the full amount of above-mentioned withholding taxes
due to the BIR by filing appropriate returns. Henceforth, strictly observe the
deadlines in the remittance of taxes withheld on compensation and government
money payments to suppliers and contractors pursuant to the above-cited
revenue regulations to avoid the incurrence of penalties and surcharges.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management agreed onthefollowing:
Forthe LocalChief Executive:
1. As the Head of the Procuring Entity, ensure that the performance of the
contractors is properly tracked to monitor compliance with performance
indicators and with all the conditions and requirements of the contract. In case
of a breach, impose the necessary penalties and sanctions as prescribed under
RA9184andrelevant provisionsofthe GPPB; and
2. Be stringent in blacklisting contractors who are found to be negligent in
performingtheirobligationas stipulated in thecontracts.
Forthe MunicipalAccountant to:
3. Review and trace the dormant accounts, ascertain the reasons for it being non-
moving, reconcile the records with the books of the source agencies and return
the excess funds, if any, and reflect adjustments in the books when necessary.
Henceforth, ensure the reliability of the reported Due to National Government
Agencies account inthefinancialstatements;

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following courses of
action totheManagement:
a) For the Local Chief Executive, Sangguniang Bayan, and responsible
departments to stop granting bonuses and incentives that are devoid of legal
basis and exceeding the prescribed rates and refrain from approving
disbursements thataretainted with irregularities;
Forthe MunicipalAccountant:
a) Ensure that all payments for personnel benefits are appropriately recorded
and classified in their proper account in the books to reflect the correct
amountof expenses.
b) Ensure that benefits and other compensation of employees and workers are
properly deducted and withheld with taxes as prescribed under NIRC and
RA10963.

With the foregoing audit observations, we recommended and Management
agreed onthefollowing:
1. For the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to formulate a
comprehensive multi-sectoral development plan that directly supports the
Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program. Henceforth,
during planning phase, the LDC should ensure that all charges against 20%
Development Plan are properly supported with specific, measurable,
attainable,realisticandtime-bounded programs,projects andactivities;
2. For the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and Local Finance Council
(LFC) to assess all savings from completed projects and evaluate the
implementability of projects that remained unimplemented for several years
and consider its reversion and re-appropriation to other priority
development projects in compliance with the guidelines on the utilization of
thefund;
3. Periodic monitoring on the implementation of development projects should
be done, specifically those in the continuing appropriations, in order not to
accumulate balances and delay the services which the intended beneficiaries
couldhaveiftimely implemented;and
4. Local budget, plans and goals shall, as far as practicable, be harmonized
with national development plans, goals and strategies in order to optimize
the utilization of resources and to avoid duplication in the use of fiscal and
physical resources.

With the foregoing observations in the planning and budgeting of the BGCM
Fund, we recommended and Management agreed for the Local Development Council
(LDC), together with the Local Finance Council (LFC), to ensure that all
appropriations are properly supported with plans. Harmonize local plans with
investmentprograms, budget,and expenditure managementprocedures.

With these,werecommended towhich theLocal ChiefExecutive agreed to:
a. Henceforth, enhance the internal control and employ additional mechanisms in
the distribution of financial aids to avoid the incurrence of delays in the future
releases andpayouts;and
b. To ensure that the inter-agency funds' utilization and corresponding liquidation
adheretothe prescribed guidelines.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive in which the Management

1. For the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee to ensure
that all programs/projects/activities (PPAs) and expenses funded by the
Municipality are incorporated in the Annual Investment Program or

2. For the Municipal Budget Office to submit justification on why a duly approved

programmed LDRRM PPAs. Henceforth, ensure that all changes in the Annual
Budget of the LGU are covered by either Budget Augmentation or

4. Submit appropriate evidence to substantiate the transactions and address the noted deficiencies.
5. Exercise due diligence and professional care in the review of documentary requirements taking into consideration the legality of the submitted documents.
For the Local Chief Executive to:
6. Regularly monitor the status of implementation of the downloaded funds as well as create a priority disbursement planning.
7. Strengthen internal control procedures in prevention, detection and correction of irregularities.

observations.recommendation:
1. Ensure that all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year. For the operating expenses, management should see to it that cash advances granted is tantamount to the activity/ies that can be accomplished within the year. Unused cash advances for travels and seminars for the year should be returned or refunded to the collecting officer at year-end.
2. Ensure that cash advances shall only be given for a legally authorized specific purpose and adequately supported with relevant supporting documents;
3. Revisit the provisions on supporting documents on the grants and liquidations of cash advances and strictly adhere thereto; and
4. Stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees until the previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The LGU conducted meeting with the SOCOTECO II. Both parties agreed that the existing post will be relocated at their expense. Thus SOCOTECO II will download funds. Further, return of unutilized transfers was not done in violation of COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
Further recommendations included: Assist Implementing agencies in securing Deed of Donations of the project sites, expedite construction of infrastructure projects, return excess funds from completed projects to the source agencies, implement timely the intended PPAs of the fund transfers so that benefits may be enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries, exert effort to determine the composition of the unreconciled amount of P614,566.50 existing in the account Due to NGAs and make appropriate adjustments after valid justifications were derived in the variance amount.

(c) Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
1. For the General Service Office to promptly prepare and submit a complete
and verified Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to
the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team for validation.
2. For the General Service Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to
annually reconcile the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting
records to establish the correct value of PPE at year end.
3. For the Municipal Accounting Office to gather necessary data to substantiate
the unreconciled balances in the Property, Plant and Equipment and
Construction in Progress accounts and provide the necessary adjusting entries,
if warranted.
4. For the Municipal Accountant to secure supporting documents and draw the
necessary adjustments for the provision and recognition of depreciation
expense for the affected PPE accounts in the books of accounts of the
Municipal Government of Maitum . Henceforth, properly recognize the
annual depreciation of all Property, Plant and Equipment based on the
guidelines set under IPSAS 17 and other relevant COA issuances.
5. For the Municipal Accounting Office to coordinate with the Municipal
Engineering Office and obtain necessary data for the proper disclosure of the
road network system as required in the Notes to Financial Statements in
accordance with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

1. For the Municipal Accountant, to implement the provisions of COA Circular
No. 96-011 and exert efforts in gathering the necessary supporting documents
to enable the recording of the long unsettled/unadjusted valid reconciling
items as reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
2. For the checks that became stale in the hands of the payee, the Municipal
Accountant should give a list to the Municipal Treasurer for information. The
Municipal Treasurer, in return should notify the payee/s to present the stale
checks for replacement and the corresponding adjustment in the affected
reports.
3. For the stale checks which have been unclaimed, the Municipal Treasurer
should cancel the checks and return the original DV and supporting
documents to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of adjusting
entries. Further, exert extra effort to secure the addresses and contact
information of the payee/s for notification.

1. Ensure that all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year.
For the operating expenses, management should see to it that cash advances
granted is tantamount to the activity/ies that can be accomplished within the
year. Unused cash advances for travels and seminars for the year should be
returned or refunded to the collecting officer at year-end.
2. Ensure that cash advances shall only be given for a legally authorized specific
purpose and adequately supported with relevant supporting documents;
3. Revisit the provisions on supporting documents on the grants and liquidations
of cash advances and strictly adhere thereto; and
4. Stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees until the
previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed on the
following:
1. For the Municipal Assessor to prepare annually the updated Assessment Roll
and provide the same to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for collection of real
property and special education tax;
2. For the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account
Register (RPTAR)/ Taxpayer’s index based on the submitted Assessment Roll
and furnish Accounting Office of the duly certified list of taxpayers, the
amount due and collectible for the year. Henceforth, such list shall be provided
at the beginning of each year;
3. For the Municipal Accountant to set-up the Real Property Tax and Special
Education Tax (RPT/SET) receivable as soon as the MTO will submit the duly
certified list and book all the necessary entries affecting the receivables and
deferred income accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed the conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and start the process of writing off the accounts. Revisit COA Circular 2016-005 for the guideline and procedures.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: Request for write-off not submitted

observations.recommendation: Establish and maintain a special account for the transactions under public utilities and other economic enterprises and the 20% Local Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting
Office refer to the General Services Office records in reporting the properties.
Determine the composition of the other unreconciled accounts. Make appropriate
adjustments in the books to attain accuracy in the presentation of the financial
statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting
Office prepare the necessary documents and submit request for write-off to COA
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects
and programs under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the
attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the
LGU; and
b. Include in the formulation of the programs, projects and activities under
the 20% LDF the savings from implemented prior year projects.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Local Finance Committee to ensure that the projects to be funded under
the 20% Local Development Fund are derived from the local development plans and
included in the approved Annual Investment Program for the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Bids and Awards Committee to comply strictly on the submission of
contracts and financial transactions/documents within the prescribed period in
accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to avoid lump sum
appropriations and ensure that the approved local development plans contains
identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting
Office maintain a special account for the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
require the Municipal Engineering Office to comply with the required posting of
information and publicity on programs/projects/activities to promote good
governance, transparency and accountability and to accord the public of their right
for information.

We recommended and Management agreed to initiate courses of action to
address the gaps in the implementation of LDRRM programs, projects and
activities to optimally utilize the available resources in LDRRM Fund. Record the
unexpended capital outlay to continuing appropriations under the General Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to fill-up the mandatory position
and create a separate budget for the LDRRM Office to enable the LGU strengthens
its capacity in disaster risk reduction and management.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting and General Services Office reconcile their records to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a. The Municipal Accountant will exert effort to prepare timely the BRS. In case of delay, find bank alternative ways to determine the balances from its depository banks and prepare BRS to ascertain the accuracy of the reported balances of its accounts. b. Prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements within the prescribed time. c. Identify the cause of variances of the general and subsidiary ledgers and book and bank balances. Prepare necessary adjustments for the differences. d. Make adjustment on the stale checks and valid reconciling items in BRS to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts. e. Reflect the unrecorded Trust Fund accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR Fully Implemented; Snapshots from the bank were obtained to prepare timely the BRS; Causes of variances were identified and adjusted in the books; Make adjustment on the stale checks and valid reconciling items in BRS to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts; Reflect the unrecorded Trust Fund accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Snapshots from the bank were obtained to prepare timely the BRS. Causes of variances were identified and adjusted in the books. Make adjustment on the stale checks and valid reconciling items in BRS to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts. Reflect the unrecorded Trust Fund accounts in the financial statements.

It is also recommended to include in the revised solid waste management plan the establishment of a sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: SLF included in 2017 plan
observations.reason:
(Reiterated)
Identified SLF Site has no title (assessment only)

observations.recommendation:
Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 19 and 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual in order to ensure reliability of the balances of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts.  Set up RPT/SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Taxpayers.
Maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) for Real Property Receivable in order to facilitate monitoring of tax collections and delinquencies.
observations.reason: They cannot record the receivable because this would affect the LGU's collection efficiency

observations.recommendation: Conduct a comprehensive study on the LGU's Personnel compliment such that PS cost shall within the limitation provided by law.
observations.reason: The LGU continuously implemented tranches of the Salary Standardization Law

observations.recommendation: LSB Resolution must be sought where realignment of Special Education Fund is necessary and before committing disbursement of funds.
Remit to the Bureau of Treasury the unutilized balances of DAP and PDAF amounting to P178,520.97 for projects that have been fully completed as well as those pertaining to the unimplemented projects.

observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation and adjustment of valid reconciling Items.
observations.reason: Voluminous Workload

observations.recommendation: Provide the necessary appropriations for Personal Services, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay for LDRRMO under the General Fund in accordance with existing regulations.

observations.managementAction: Demands were sent, monthly deduction of unliquidated cash advances were made and restored amount which was close at year end
